mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP signals underlying aggregate demand expansion, which translates into higher expected corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of GDPC1 growth falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and corporate borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly elevated federal funds rates and consumer price index levels reflect a restrictive monetary environment with high inflation, eroding real corporate earnings and compressing multiples to generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to achieve a Rank IC magnitude above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market momentum that precedes aggregate demand contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered observations during late-cycle periods, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over a 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a contraction in manufacturing output and aggregate supply, leading to negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 50% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in housing starts and retail sales captures a broad-based demand impulse from both residential investment and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS yields a t-statistic below 1.5 in predictive regressions over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls signals an inflection in aggregate employment and income growth, leading to reduced consumption expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns following a monthly PAYEMS decline in more than 45% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand that drives revenue misses across consumer discretionary sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months following a month-over-month decline in RSAFS.
